description: Add OAuth 2.0 browser-based login for GitLab, supporting both gitlab.com AND arbitrary self-hosted GitLab instances (e.g. git.intrepidcs.net). Uses PKCE flow for public desktop clients with no client secret. Users paste their GitLab OAuth Application ID into the Add Account form, click 'Login with GitLab', authorize in the browser, and come back to a logged-in account. PAT flow continues to work unchanged as a fallback.

userStories.title: OAuth PKCE helpers and loopback callback server
userStories.description: As a developer, I want main-process helpers to generate PKCE code verifiers/challenges and to briefly run a localhost HTTP server that captures the OAuth authorization code redirect, so that later stories can compose them into a full OAuth flow.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/main/gitlab-oauth.ts exports helpers: generatePkcePair() returning { verifier, challenge } using SHA-256 (base64url-encoded), and startLoopbackServer(port: number) returning a Promise<{ code: string, state: string }> that resolves when /callback is hit with ?code=... query parameters
generatePkcePair uses crypto.randomBytes(32) for the verifier (base64url, no padding), and crypto.createHash('sha256').update(verifier).digest('base64url') for the challenge
startLoopbackServer listens on exactly http.createServer().listen(47823, '127.0.0.1') — fixed port so the redirect_uri registered with GitLab always matches
If the port is already in use, the server rejects with a clear error: 'Port 47823 is already in use — close the app using it and try again'

On GET /callback, the server: (1) reads ?code and ?state from the URL, (2) responds with a minimal HTML page saying 'GitSlop login successful — you can close this tab', (3) closes itself after a 500ms delay to ensure the browser received the response, (4) resolves the Promise with { code, state }
On GET /callback with ?error=... (user denied), the server responds with an error HTML page and rejects the Promise with the error description
The server times out and rejects after 120 seconds if no callback arrives

Add a unit test in src/main/__tests__/gitlab-oauth.test.ts: verifies generatePkcePair produces distinct verifier/challenge pairs, that challenge === base64url(sha256(verifier)), and that the verifier+challenge are URL-safe (no +/= chars)
Typecheck and existing tests still pass
userStories.notes: crypto is built into Node, no new deps. Use 'http' module for the loopback server — don't reach for express. Port 47823 is arbitrary but must match what the user registers as their OAuth app's Redirect URI. Document this constant clearly so future maintainers understand why it can't change casually. GitLab's OAuth docs: https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/api/oauth2.html

userStories.title: End-to-end OAuth flow — authorize, exchange code, return user + token
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click a single 'Login with GitLab' button, authorize GitSlop in my browser, and return to find a new GitLab account added, so that I don't have to manually create and paste a Personal Access Token.
acceptanceCriteria: src/main/gitlab-oauth.ts exports an async function startOAuthFlow({ instanceUrl, clientId }) that: (1) generates a PKCE pair, (2) generates a random state token, (3) constructs the GitLab authorize URL: {instanceUrl}/oauth/authorize?client_id={id}&redirect_uri=http://localhost:47823/callback&response_type=code&state={state}&scope=api+read_user&code_challenge={challenge}&code_challenge_method=S256, (4) opens it in the user's default browser via electron.shell.openExternal, (5) starts the loopback server and awaits the callback, (6) verifies the returned state matches, (7) POSTs to {instanceUrl}/oauth/token with grant_type=authorization_code, code, client_id, redirect_uri, code_verifier, (8) parses the JSON response { access_token, refresh_token, expires_in, token_type }, (9) calls GET /api/v4/user with the new access token to fetch the user profile, (10) returns { user, accessToken, refreshToken, expiresAt } where expiresAt = Date.now() + expires_in * 1000

New IPC handler gitlab:startOAuthFlow in src/main/index.ts that accepts { instanceUrl, clientId, label? } from the renderer, calls startOAuthFlow, then stores the result as a new IntegrationAccount with authType: 'oauth' and the refresh token fields alongside the encrypted access token (see US-GL-003). Returns the account info (same shape as gitlab:addAccount's return value) so the UI can render the new row without reloading
If the user closes the browser without authorizing, the loopback server's 120s timeout fires and the IPC handler returns { success: false, error: 'OAuth login timed out or was cancelled' }
If the state mismatches or the token exchange fails, the IPC handler returns { success: false, error: <specific error message> } — do not crash
Preload: expose window.electronAPI.gitlab.startOAuthFlow(opts) wrapping the IPC
Type definitions in src/renderer/src/hooks/useLayoutState.ts gitlab: block include startOAuthFlow
Typecheck passes, existing tests still pass
userStories.notes: electron.shell.openExternal is already imported in main/index.ts — use it. Use the existing gitlabApiRequest helper for the /api/v4/user call. Use raw https for the /oauth/token POST since it's not under /api/v4. GitLab's POST /oauth/token accepts application/x-www-form-urlencoded OR application/json — use JSON for consistency. The response body includes 'created_at' as a unix timestamp in seconds AND 'expires_in' in seconds; compute expiresAt from Date.now() + expires_in*1000 to avoid clock-skew headaches.

userStories.title: Extended IntegrationAccount with refresh-token auto-refresh
userStories.description: As a user, I want my GitLab OAuth session to automatically refresh when the access token expires, so that I don't have to re-authorize every few hours.
acceptanceCriteria: IntegrationAccount interface in src/main/index.ts gains optional fields: authType?: 'pat' | 'oauth' (default 'pat'), refreshToken?: string (encrypted via encryptToken), expiresAt?: number (ms since epoch), clientId?: string (OAuth only — needed to refresh)

New helper refreshGitLabToken(account: IntegrationAccount): Promise<IntegrationAccount | null> in src/main/gitlab-oauth.ts that: (1) decrypts the stored refresh token, (2) POSTs to {account.instanceUrl}/oauth/token with grant_type=refresh_token, refresh_token, client_id, (3) on success returns a cloned account with new encrypted access+refresh tokens and updated expiresAt, (4) on failure (refresh token expired or revoked) returns null

New helper ensureFreshToken(account) in gitlab-oauth.ts: if account.authType === 'oauth' and account.expiresAt is within 60 seconds of now (or already past), calls refreshGitLabToken, persists the updated account to electron-store, returns the decrypted access token. If authType === 'pat' or no expiresAt, returns the decrypted account.token as-is (unchanged behavior for existing PAT accounts)

All existing gitlab:* IPC handlers (getAccounts, listMergeRequests, getMergeRequest, createMergeRequest, etc.) that currently decrypt account.token must instead call ensureFreshToken(account) to get a guaranteed-valid token

If ensureFreshToken returns null (refresh failed), the account is kept in the store but the IPC handler returns { success: false, error: 'GitLab session expired — please re-authorize' } and marks the account row with an 'error' field so the UI shows a 'Re-login' hint
Legacy PAT accounts (no authType field) continue to work unchanged — the authType check treats undefined as 'pat'
Typecheck passes, existing tests still pass
userStories.notes: Be careful with the atomic read-modify-write of the account list in electron-store. After refreshGitLabToken returns the updated account, re-read gitlabAccounts from the store, replace the matching id, and write it back. Do NOT serialize the in-memory account back to the store since other operations may have written to it concurrently. GitLab refresh tokens are single-use — each refresh returns a NEW refresh_token that replaces the old one. If you don't persist the new refresh token, the next refresh will fail.

userStories.title: Settings UI — OAuth tab in Add GitLab Account form
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Add GitLab Account form to offer two login methods (OAuth browser login and Personal Access Token), with clear setup instructions for self-hosted instances, so that I can pick whichever matches my GitLab instance's capabilities.
acceptanceCriteria: GitLabSection in src/renderer/src/components/SettingsPanel.tsx (around line 1537) gains a method tab at the top of the Add Account form: two buttons 'OAuth' and 'Personal Access Token', styled similarly to the existing view-mode toggles elsewhere in the app

OAuth tab contents: Instance URL input (defaults to https://gitlab.com), Account Label input, Client ID / Application ID input with a hint 'Paste the Application ID from your GitLab OAuth app', a collapsible 'How to register an OAuth app' section that expands to show step-by-step instructions, and a 'Login with GitLab' button

The collapsible instructions read: 'On your GitLab instance, go to User Settings → Applications → Add new application. Name: GitSlop. Redirect URI: http://localhost:47823/callback (exactly this, including the port). Confidential: UNCHECKED. Scopes: api, read_user. Save and paste the Application ID above.' Include a clickable 'Open applications page' link that launches {instanceUrl}/-/user_settings/applications in the user's browser

Clicking 'Login with GitLab' disables the button, shows a 'Opening browser, waiting for authorization…' status, and calls window.electronAPI.gitlab.startOAuthFlow({ instanceUrl, clientId, label }). On success, clears the form, reloads the accounts list, and closes the add form. On failure, shows the error message inline
PAT tab contents are unchanged — same form, same behavior as current
The two tabs remember their own form state while the user switches between them (don't reset instanceUrl when switching tabs)
A small one-line note above the tabs: 'OAuth requires registering an application on your GitLab instance. PAT works on any instance without setup.'

Typecheck passes. Manually verify in browser via `npm run dev` that both tabs render, tabs can be switched, OAuth instructions expand/collapse, and the 'Open applications page' link opens the correct URL
userStories.notes: Reuse the existing .ghInput, .ghLoginBtn, .ghLoginSection CSS classes. Add a new .ghMethodTabs class for the tab row if needed. The collapsible instructions can be a simple <details>/<summary> or a React useState toggle — either works. The OAuth flow is opt-in so keep PAT as the default tab for discoverability (users who haven't registered an app shouldn't land on a form that needs a Client ID they don't have).
